title: 解释论文中的核心方法

messages.text: 解释论文中的核心方法

📌 核心要点：论文在第3页定义了三种开放式的复杂事件预测任务（短期、长期、论元级），并介绍了为支持这些任务而构建的OpenForecast数据集及其收集流程。

● 任务定义方面，论文首先将复杂事件CE定义为同一主题下按时间顺序排列的事件链，即“$C E = \{ e _ { 1 } , e _ { 2 } . . . e _ { n } \}$”【第3页】。时间戳$T_k$将事件链划分为背景事件$X$和目标事件$Y$。基于此，论文设计了三种开放式预测任务：
  ① 短期预测（STF）：给定时间戳$T_k$，模型需预测该时刻发生的所有多方面原子事件【第3页】。
  ② 长期预测（LTF）：模型需预测$T_k$之后的多步事件链演化【第3页】。
  ③ 论元级问答（AQA）：模型需回答关于事件类型、主体、客体、时间、地点等细粒度论元的问题，例如“Who will”对应答案“Ukraine”【第3页】。

● 此外，论文还提出了三种封闭式任务：
  ① 多选叙事填空（MCNC）：从四个候选事件中选出正确的一个【第3页】。
  ② 多选论元填空（MCAC）：从四个候选答案中选出正确的论元【第3页】。
  ③ 验证问答（VQA）：判断给定候选事件是否会发生【第3页】。

● 数据集构建方面，论文指出当前缺乏支持开放式任务的数据集，因此提出了OpenForecast数据集。其构建流程包括三个步骤：“(1) dataset collection for complex events; (2) event timeline annotation; (3) question generation”【第3页】。数据来源为Wikipedia和Wikipedia Current Events Portal（WCEP），经过多步过滤去除重复、非事件和非当代数据后，将同一主题的文章分组，形成大规模高质量复杂事件集合【第3页】。

---
💡 提示：第3页主要聚焦任务定义与数据收集框架，后续章节（如第4节）可能详细展开数据标注与生成的具体细节。

To facilitate the unforeseen, multifaceted, and multi-step event forecasting, we design a shortterm and a long-term forecasting tasks, which predict events on a specific date and l

Long-Term Forecasting (LTF). This task examines the ability to forecast long-term event evolution after a given timestamp $T _ { k }$ . With event background X as input, models are
Argument-level QA (AQA). This task examines the forecasting ability on fine-grained event arguments, including event type, subject, object, time, and location. In this task, models
Based on existing closed-ended tasks, we also propose three closed-ended tasks:
Multi-Choice Narrative Cloze (MCNC). Similar to script event prediction, models are provided with event background X and four subsequent candidate events, with one gold answer insi
Multi-Choice Argument-level Cloze (MCAC). Similar to AQA, four candidate answers are additionally provided, with one gold answer inside.
Verify QA (VQA). In this task, given the event background and one candidate event, models need to predict whether the candidate event will occur.
We review current event forecasting datasets and identify a lack of datasets for the open-ended tasks. To support these tasks above, we present OpenForecast, a large-scale dataset.
